'''Charly Runciman''' (born 22 July 1993) is an Australian professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|ce}} and {{rlp|wg}}er for the [[St. George Illawarra Dragons]] in the [[Canterbury Cup NSW]].

He previously played for the [[St. George Illawarra Dragons]] in the [[National Rugby League|NRL]] and the [[Widnes Vikings]] in the [[Super League]].

==Background==
Born in [[Dubbo|Dubbo, New South Wales]], Runciman played his junior football for the St Johns and [[Dubbo CYMS]], before being signed by the [[St. George Illawarra Dragons]].

==Playing career==
===Early career===
In 2012 and 2013, Runciman played for the St. George Illawarra Dragons' [[National Youth Competition (rugby league)|NYC]] team.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://nycdatabase.org/players/r/|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150630065823/http://nycdatabase.org/players/r/|url-status=usurped|archive-date=30 June 2015|title=R|work=NYC DATABASE}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://rleague.com/players/Charly-Runciman_3499/Matches/|accessdate=4 July 2015|url-status=dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20130924191649/http://rleague.com/players/Charly-Runciman_3499/Matches/|archivedate=24 September 2013|title=Charly Runciman player results, scores, news and statistics}}</ref> At the end of 2012, he was named the Dragons' NYC Player of the Year.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.dragons.com.au/team/profiles/charly_runciman.html|title=Charly Runciman|publisher=Dragons.com.au|accessdate=2015-07-09|url-status=dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20150705174018/http://www.dragons.com.au/team/profiles/charly_runciman.html|archivedate=5 July 2015 }}</ref> On 20 April 2013, he played for the [[New South Wales rugby league team|New South Wales]] under-20s team against the [[Queensland rugby league team|Queensland]] under-20s team.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.nrl.com/under-20s-origin-squads-named/tabid/10874/newsid/71757/default.aspx|title=Under 20's Origin squads named|publisher=National Rugby League|accessdate=2015-03-11}}</ref>

===2013===
In Round 16 of the [[2013 NRL season]], Runciman made his [[National Rugby League|NRL]] debut for the Dragons against the [[Penrith Panthers]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.dragons.com.au/news-display/Late-Mail-Round-16/78280|accessdate=4 July 2015|url-status=dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20130702051901/http://www.dragons.com.au/news-display/Late-Mail-Round-16/78280|archivedate=2 July 2013|title=Late Mail: Round 16|publisher=St. George Illawarra Dragons}}</ref> On 27 August 2013, he was named at centre in the 2013 NYC Team of the Year.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.nrl.com/2013-holden-cup-team-of-the-year/tabid/10874/newsid/74260/default.aspx|title=2013 Holden Cup Team of the Year|publisher=National Rugby League|date=2013-08-27|accessdate=2015-05-05}}</ref> On 13 October 2013, he played for the [[Junior Kangaroos]] against the [[Junior Kiwis]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.nrl.com/junior-kangaroos-team-named/tabid/10874/newsid/75007/default.aspx|title=Junior Kangaroos team named|publisher=National Rugby League|date=2013-10-07|accessdate=2015-06-27}}</ref>

===2014===
On 23 May 2014, Runciman re-signed with the Dragons on a 2-year contract.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.dragons.com.au/news/2014/05/23/runciman_to_remain_a_dragon.html|title=Runciman To Remain A Dragon|publisher=Dragons.com.au|accessdate=2015-07-09}}</ref>

===2015===
On 21 July 2015, Runciman signed a {{frac|2|1|2}}-year contract with [[Super League]] team [[Widnes Vikings|Widnes]] effective immediately, after being granted a release from the final {{frac|1|1|2}} years of his Dragons contract.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.dragons.com.au/news/2015/07/21/dragons_grant_runcim.html|title=Grant Runciman Release|publisher=Dragons.com.au|accessdate=2015-07-22}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.zerotackle.com/nrl/dragons-grant-runciman-release-to-join-vikings-18275/|title=Dragons grant Runciman release to join Vikings | NRL|publisher=Zero Tackle|accessdate=2015-07-22}}</ref>
